SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.punishment

Interface Punishment

All Superinterfaces:
ReasonHolder
All Known Subinterfaces:
Ban, Kick, Mute, TemporalPunishment
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractPunishment, AbstractTemporalPunishment, NecrifyBan, NecrifyKick, NecrifyMute, VelocityKick
public interface Punishment extends ReasonHolder
Super interface for all sort of punishments..
To punish a player, obtain a NecrifyUser instance from UserManager and select an adequate method (e.g. NecrifyUser.banPermanent(Component)) for the punishment you wish.

    createFullReason, getReason

  • Method Details

    • isOngoing

      boolean isOngoing()
      Determines whether the expiration of this punishment is after LocalDateTime.now() or not.
      Returns:
      true, if the expiration date is after now, otherwise false

    • punish

      CompletableFuture<Punishment> punish()
      Punishes the player finally.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the exerted punishment
      Throws:
      PunishmentException - if the punishment could not be exerted (inside the future)

    • cancel

      CompletableFuture<Punishment> cancel()
      Cancels this punishment thus allowing the player e.g. to join the server
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the cancelled punishment
      Throws:
      PunishmentException - if the punishment could not be cancelled (inside the future)

    • change

      CompletableFuture<Punishment> change(@Nullable @Nullable net.kyori.adventure.text.Component newReason)
      Changes the duration and reason of this punishment. This method can be used if a player created an appeal an it was accepted.
      Parameters:
      newReason - the new reason which should be displayed to the player or null if the reason should not be changed
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the new punishment
      Throws:
      PunishmentException - if the punishment could not be changed
      See Also:
      • cancel()

    • getType

      @NotNull @NotNull PunishmentType getType()
      Returns the type of this punishment. By default, this is a field from StandardPunishmentType.
      Returns:
      the type of this punishment
      See Also:
      • StandardPunishmentType

    • getPunishmentUuid

      @NotNull @NotNull UUID getPunishmentUuid()
      Returns:
      the id of this punishment.

    • getUuid

      @NotNull @NotNull UUID getUuid()
      This will return the value of NecrifyUser.getUuid() from getUser().
      Returns:
      the uuid of the punished player.
      Since:
      1.0.1

    • getUser

      @NotNull @NotNull NecrifyUser getUser()
      Returns:
      the user this punishment is affecting
      Since:
      1.2.0

    • hasSuccessor

      boolean hasSuccessor()
      Returns whether this punishment has a successor punishment.
      Returns:
      true, if this punishment has a successor punishment, otherwise false
      Since:
      1.2.0
      See Also:
      • getSuccessor()

    • getSuccessor

      @NotNull @NotNull Punishment getSuccessor()
      Returns the successor of this punishment. This is used to chain punishments so that one punishment of the same kind is paused until the previous one is finished. This may especially be useful for punishments of differing reasons.
      Returns:
      the successor of this punishment
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if there is no successor punishment - check hasSuccessor()
      UnsupportedOperationException - if the underlying punishment does not support succeeding punishments (e.g. Kicks)
      Since:
      1.2.0
      See Also:
      • hasSuccessor()

    • setSuccessor

      void setSuccessor(@NotNull @NotNull Punishment successor)
      Sets the successor of this punishment. This is used to chain punishments so that one punishment of the same kind is paused until the previous one is finished. This may especially be useful for punishments of differing reasons.
      Parameters:
      successor - the successor of this punishment
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if the underlying punishment does not support succeeding punishments (e.g. Kicks)
